reasoning: SPY at 740.26, consolidating just below the 5-day Carry high at 740.73 (+$0.47 away). Overnight gap-up (+2.07) has been held in premarket. Ribbon is BULL stack (Fast 739.99, Pivot 739.81, Slow 738.63, spread 135c) with no inversion. The battle level is 740.73 — the immediate overhead resistance and the 5-day RTH high. If 740.73 holds on a test from below, it confirms a bullish floor. Below, 738.84 (yesterday's high) and 738.18 (premarket low) provide tight support. Asymmetry favors upside: room to 743.3 (Pivot R2) vs tight stop to 738.18. Entry triggers align with level hold + ribbon support.

primary.condition: if SPY holds 740.73 on first test (likely within 09:45-10:15 ET as price consolidates toward resistance)
primary.entry_trigger: level hold + Fast EMA (739.99) support confirmed + ribbon stack intact

secondary.condition: if SPY breaks above 740.73 with volume

tertiary.condition: if SPY rejects 740.73 and pulls back below Fast EMA (739.99)

key_observations: Gap-up +2.07 (738.19 → 740.26) held through premarket — no gap-fill urgency yet.
Price consolidating in tight range (738.18 to 740.92 premarket) directly below 5-day high 740.73 — first real resistance is only 47 bps away.
Ribbon BULL (spread 135c), no inversion, Fast EMA 739.99 acts as effective support floor — price is ~26bps above Fast EMA.
Immediate support at 738.84 (yesterday RTH high) and 738.18 (PML) both within $1.50 — risk/reward asymmetry favors bullish entries.
VIX rising (18.09, +0.56%) despite gap-up — typical short-covering or hedge unwind into strength; not panic.
No multi-timeframe divergence visible in premarket — ribbon and price structure aligned.
